summary: Card created (todo/planned). USER TESTS: install the shipped 1.1.0 build on a clean machine and generate on the LOCAL engine. ROOT = the two-file engine-version desync (memory: project_release_engine_version_desync). node_lock.json core.tag went v0.26->v0.27 in 6884514b (MPI-148, 2026-07-03); system_dependencies.json engine.version went 0.26->0.27 only in 41c59093, the 1.1.0 release commit itself. merge-base --is-ancestor confirms node_lock was already 0.27 at the cut. The local gate (routes/engine.js:509 /engine/version-check) compares .mpi_engine_version to COMFY_VERSION from system_dependencies.json => during all 1.1.0 testing both said 0.26 => local engine NEVER ran 0.27. The Pod reads node_lock, so RunPod (which IS the 1.1.0 release) ran 0.27 throughout. Bounded gap: LOCAL engine on 0.27 is untested, NOT an untested release. INCIDENTAL: agent launched the app on the bumped branch, firing the first 0.26->0.27 local upgrade (~2GB + 11 node re-pins). Parallel installer RACED - python install.py hit comfyui-frame-interpolation before extraction finished (Errno 2, exit 2), aborting the batch and leaving Impact-Subpack + RES4LYF missing. USER PRESSED RETRY, RECOVERED (7/11 -> 9/11, clean boot). Race != 0.27 incompat; if it repeats on a clean install it needs its own card. Per feedback_runpod_not_local_engine_proof a RunPod run does NOT verify this.

Picked up: verifying 1.1.0 local engine on 0.27. Session found + fixed the first-boot node-install race family: (1) stale-zip scrub in ResumableDownloader.download() stops NDH ' (1)' duplicate zips (RES4LYF (1).zip etc); (2) a per-dep requirements failure in _runCustomNodeInstall now sets anyFailure+continue instead of throw, so a frame-interpolation install.py hiccup no longer aborts the batch and strands Impact-Subpack+RES4LYF. Test tests/node-install-batch-resilience.test.cjs (pass). ALSO scoping a 1.1.1 master patch: port MPI-245 (c03c99f6 missing-LoRA toast, covers the SDXL Liora_Lustify7 value_not_in_list dialog) + MPI-246 (aa958869 Ultralytics auto-mask heal). triton ModuleNotFound = benign (MPI-50 intentional, KJNodes still loads). RIFE re-download on reboot = first-boot race consequence (file was in the aborted batch, never landed; reboot correctly fetched it), NOT a marker orphan (disk: rife47.pth present, no .cubricdl).

ROOT CAUSE found + fixed (db333558 on 1.2.0, cherry-picked 5619d41d to master for 1.1.1). The 'UW deps installation failed / Press Retry' on a fresh Windows install was NOT a race: a targetPath weight (RIFE ckpts/rife/rife47.pth resolves UNDER custom_nodes/comfyui-frame-interpolation/) downloads BEFORE the node extracts, creating the node folder as a subdir-only SHELL. Then `alreadyExtracted = fs.pathExists(targetDir)` false-positived -> skipped extraction -> `python install.py` in a folder with no install.py -> Errno 2. Even when extraction ran, the rename block removed the freshly-extracted node as a 'duplicate' of the shell. Fix (order-independent): new `_nodeFolderHasFiles()` keys both checks on top-level FILES not folder-exists; rename block MERGES the node into a weight-shell (fs.copy overwrite + remove source) preserving ckpts/. Two SECONDARY fixes same card (b239693e): stale-zip scrub (no ` (1)` dups) + per-dep reqs failure = anyFailure+continue not throw (batch no longer strands later deps). Those two made Retry RECOVER; the weight-shell fix removes the need to Retry. Tests: tests/node-install-batch-resilience.test.cjs (4 cases). SHIPPED in 1.1.1 (master pushed, rebuilt). memory: project_targetpath_weight_shell_trap.

DONE + user-verified live + SHIPPED in 1.1.1. Windows clean-install: all 11 nodes installed with NO Retry (weight-shell root fix db333558/5619d41d), gen/mask/upscale/Chroma/PiD/SDXL all clean. 4 fixes shipped: MPI-245 (LoRA toast), MPI-246 (auto-mask heal), MPI-243 batch-resilience+zip-scrub, MPI-243 weight-shell root. master 5619d41d pushed+built; 1.2.0 carries all + UNRELEASED note; R2 link vision/v1.1-d690d5f7711d13716691 swapped to 1.1.1 (old v1.1.0 files removed). Linux/mac run-test deferred to MPI-249.
